message:
fix(unix): only remove stale socket files during UnixListener::bind, refuse non-socket paths

The previous bind() implementation blindly called remove_file() on the
target path before binding, which would silently delete regular files,
directories, or symlinks that happened to occupy the path — a data loss
risk in misconfigured deployments.

Replaced with remove_stale_socket_file() which:
- Checks symlink_metadata to inspect the file type without following
  symlinks
- Only removes files whose type is_socket() (S_IFSOCK)
- Returns AlreadyExists for non-socket paths so callers get a clear
  error instead of silent data destruction
- Treats NotFound as success (no stale socket to clean)

Added two tests:
- test_bind_refuses_non_socket_path: verifies bind returns AlreadyExists
  and preserves the non-socket file
- test_bind_replaces_stale_socket_file: verifies a leftover socket from
  a previous process is cleaned up and rebind succeeds

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
